verified_fixes: Hover cards on touch devices — VERIFIED ✅ (handleClick checks window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches and toggles pinnedCard state; card shows when hovered || pinnedCard)
Worker filter buttons overflow on mobile — VERIFIED ✅ (activity-filter-bar now has flex-wrap: wrap + gap: 6px in CSS)
Timer count reduced — PARTIAL ✅ (103 → 55 total setTimeout/setInterval calls; App.jsx: 23, Office.jsx: 32)
carry_overs: Bloberto hardcoded to never receive ping reactions — STRIKE 3 (Carlos)
activityLog.filter() called 4× inline per render — STRIKE 3 (Carlos)
WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES temporal dead zone risk — STRIKE 3 (Carlos)
Character name labels 0.44rem unreadable on mobile — STRIKE 3 (Maya)
Idle micro-bubble Date.now() expression in render — STRIKE 3 (Carlos)
Vibe constants scattered across 3 files — STRIKE 2 (Carlos)
Dashboard solo worker card layout jump — STRIKE 2 (Maya)
No deep-linking to specific worker card — STRIKE 2 (Dave)
Vibe sparkline re-reads localStorage redundantly — STRIKE 2 (Carlos)

suggestions.title: Bloberto hardcoded to never receive ping reactions — and now not even clickable
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1543: <Character worker={bloberto} ... pingReaction={null} /> — pingReaction is STILL hardcoded null. Worse: there is no onClick prop passed either, so clicking Bloberto does nothing at all. The handleCharClick in Office.jsx checks PING_REACTIONS[worker.id] which has entries for all 5 workers but not 'bloberto'. Fix both: (1) add onClick={handleCharClick} to the Bloberto Character, (2) add PING_REACTIONS.bloberto entries (a set of manager one-liners), (3) remove the hardcoded pingReaction={null} and wire it up the same way as workers.

suggestions.title: activityLog.filter() called 4× inline per render — still not memoized
suggestions.description: The activity log section runs 4 total filter passes on every render. Lines 1346-1352: activityLog.filter().filter() for the count display. Lines 1403-1412: activityLog.filter().filter() passed to ActivityLog as a prop. None are wrapped in useMemo. With a 50-entry log and 5 workers, this is 200+ comparisons per render cycle triggered by any state change. Extract into: const filteredActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(type).filter(worker), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]) and use that for both the count span and the ActivityLog entries prop.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES temporal dead zone risk — Office.jsx forward reference
suggestions.description: const Character = memo(function Character({...}) {...}) is defined at Office.jsx line 537. const WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES = {...} is defined at Office.jsx line 1138 — 600 lines AFTER Character. Character references WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES inside a useEffect closure at line 595. Works today because closures run post-module-init. Will explode the moment someone moves Character to its own file (which they should — it's 280 lines). Move all const lookup tables (WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES, WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES, DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES, AWAY_MESSAGES, WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES, PING_REACTIONS) to the top of the file or a dedicated constants module.

suggestions.title: Footer quote cycling silently dead-ends — modulo added for display, not for cycling
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1425: setQuoteBonus(p => p < BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? p + 1 : p). When quoteBonus reaches 8 (BONUS_TAGLINES.length), it stops incrementing. The display uses % correctly so the last item keeps showing, but the counter badge disappears (the check is quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length) and clicking does nothing. The user has no idea they've hit the wall. Fix: use modulo for the increment too — setQuoteBonus(p => (p + 1) % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) or just remove the guard and let it cycle infinitely with % on display. The 'fix' this cycle added the display modulo but forgot the increment guard.

suggestions.title: Character name labels at 0.44rem still fail WCAG minimum text size
suggestions.description: Office.css line 1101: .char__name { font-size: 0.44rem } at max-width: 600px. Line 1163: font-size: 0.45rem at max-width: 480px. Both are approximately 7px — below WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.4 minimum. Confirmed visible in mobile screenshot: names like 'Sofia', 'Carlos' are illegible pinhead text. WCAG minimum is 9px. Bump to at minimum 0.6rem (9.6px) at mobile breakpoints, or hide names entirely and rely on the hover/tap card (which now works on touch — good job on that fix, by the way).

suggestions.title: App.jsx and Office.jsx are still monolith files (1432 and 1660 lines)
suggestions.description: Nothing changed. Office.jsx grew to 1660 lines (up from ~1400 in review #72). The Character component alone is ~280 lines. Extract: Character → src/components/Character.jsx, WorkerCard → src/components/WorkerCard.jsx, ActivityLog+ActivityLogEntry → src/components/ActivityLog.jsx, StatsBar → src/components/StatsBar.jsx, ConferenceTable → src/components/ConferenceTable.jsx. Each new file adds context isolation that would also catch the TDZ issues during development.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast still use inline style objects
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1307-1331 (plantToast) and App.jsx lines 1157-1181 (completionToast) both use multi-line inline style objects for fixed UI elements. These are rendered conditionally but never change structure — there is no reason they need inline styles. Extract to .plant-toast and .completion-toast CSS classes. The border-color can stay dynamic via CSS custom property (e.g., style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.color }}). This has been called out twice. The inline styles prevent theming, tooling inspection, and make the JSX harder to read.

suggestions.title: Idle micro-bubble emoji picker is a one-liner nightmare
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 786: {(WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES)[Math.floor((Date.now() / 12000) + (worker.id ? [...worker.id].reduce((h, c) => h * 31 + c.charCodeAt(0), 0) : 0)) % (WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES).length]} — This runs on every render, allocates a spread array from worker.id string on every call, and is completely unreadable. Extract to: function getIdleBubble(workerId, nowMs) { const pool = WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[workerId] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES; const seed = [...workerId].reduce((h, c) => h * 31 + c.charCodeAt(0), 0); return pool[Math.floor(nowMs / 12000 + seed) % pool.length]; } — called with worker.id and a tick value.

suggestions.title: NEW: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS object literal defined inside .map() callback
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1572-1578: const WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS = { carlos: [...], maya: [...], ... } is defined INSIDE the idleWorkers.map() callback. This entire 5-key object literal (with 3 strings per key = ~15 string allocations) is recreated on every render for every idle worker. If there are 3 idle workers, that's 3 full object allocations per render cycle. Move it to module scope alongside the other lookup tables (AWAY_MESSAGES, etc.).

suggestions.title: NEW: window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)') called inline in JSX render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1212: the pollingPaused pill button renders {window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches ? 'Tap to refresh' : 'Move mouse to refresh'}. This calls window.matchMedia() on every render cycle while polling is paused. While not catastrophic, it's an unnecessary DOM API call in render. Hoist to a module-level constant: const IS_TOUCH = window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches or use a useRef computed once on mount.

suggestions.title: activeWorkers.filter() called twice inline for the same working count
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1279-1280: activeWorkers.filter((w) => w.status === 'working').length appears TWICE in the same JSX block — once for the condition and once for the count display. Both are inline with no memoization. Assign to a variable: const workingCount = activeWorkers.filter(w => w.status === 'working').length (or reuse the value from StatsBar's already-computed counts) and reference it twice.

suggestions.title: Vibe constants scattered across 3 files
suggestions.description: VIBE_RANK, VIBE_COLORS, VIBE_HISTORY_KEY in App.jsx; BLOBERTO_TITLES, VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS, VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS in Office.jsx; VIBE_WHITEBOARD in constants.js. Five separate vibe-related lookup objects across three files. Consolidate to src/utils/vibe.js alongside the existing getTeamVibe/getTeamVibeKey functions. The scattered state makes it impossible to audit all vibe-related content in one place.

suggestions.title: Dashboard solo worker card layout jump
suggestions.description: When transitioning from 2+ workers to 1 active worker, the workers-grid--solo layout (centered, spotlight message) does not activate until fading workers finish their 400ms exit animation. During that 400ms, the solo card is left-aligned in the regular grid, then jumps to center. Add a CSS transition on the grid's justify-content or delay the solo layout detection until fadingMap is also empty (which it already checks: Object.keys(fadingMap).length === 0 on line 1286 — verify this condition is truly reactive).

suggestions.title: No deep-linking to specific worker card
suggestions.description: Tab deep-linking via hash works (#office, #dashboard) but you cannot link to a specific worker. The focusedWorker mechanism is already implemented and works (scrolls to card, adds .worker-card--focused class with 3s timeout). Extend it: on mount, check URL for ?worker=sofia and call setFocusedWorker(workerParam). On handleWorkerClick, optionally append ?worker={id} to the hash URL. This would make sharing a specific worker's status possible.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline re-reads localStorage on every vibe change
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1073-1080: on every vibe key change, localStorage.getItem(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY) + JSON.parse runs, even though the parsed result is immediately pushed to and stored in vibeHistory state. The state IS the source of truth — use it. Replace the localStorage read with: const history = [...vibeHistory, { key: teamVibe.key, ts: Date.now() }].slice(-VIBE_HISTORY_MAX); setVibeHistory(history); localStorage.setItem(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY, JSON.stringify(history)); No read needed.
